calendar.matters:
Petition To Vacate Arbitration Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Petition To Vacate Arbitration Hearing is a Duplicate entry.

Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Complaint And Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATTHEW K. EDLING OF THE COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY, LLP, TEL. NO. (650)697-6000 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. ROBERT P. VARIAN OF ORRICK LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 773-5934 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS MASON LIVINGSTON AND VLADMIR DRUSKIN's Hearing On Petitioners Complaint And Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is Denied. "In order to challenge an award in court, a litigant must have raised the point before the arbitrator." (Comerica Bank v. Howsam (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 790, 829.) Otherwise, the issue is waived. (Zazueta v. Cnty. of San Benito (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 106, 111.) Here, Petitioners argue that the arbitrator erred by basing his decision on cases applying the FAA, and not California law. Petitioners did not raise this argument at the arbitration. In fact, Petitioners argued in their arbitration brief that "[w]ithin the broad, federal boundaries established by Bazzle, Stolt-Nielsen, and Oxford Health, the Arbitrator should be guided by state principles of contract law in determining whether the Arbitration Clause allows for class arbitration." (Talarides Decl., Ex. D, Arbitration brief, p. 5.) This is precisely what the Arbitrator did in rendering his decision. (See Talarides Decl., Ex. A, Arbitration Award, pp. 4-8.) Further, Petitioners take issue with the fact that the agreement in the two primary cases relied on by the Arbitrator, Kinecta Alternative Financial Solutions, Inc. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 506 and Nelsen v. Legacy Partners Residential, Inc. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1115, expressly invoked the FAA. Petitioners' reply arbitration brief makes clear that this argument was not advanced in the arbitration. Rather, Petitioners argued at the arbitration that Kinecta and Nelsen are distinguishable because the arbitration clause is broader in this case and Oxford Health Plans LLC v. Sutter (2013) 133 S.Ct. 2064 limited the Stolt-Nielsen holding and therefore, limited the application of Kinecta and Nelsen. (Talarides Decl., Ex. E, Reply arbitration brief, pp. 9-10.) Further, Garden Fresh Restaurant Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 678, cited in Petitioners' reply brief, is distinguishable. There, the parties presented the issue of whether class arbitration was available to the Court via a petition to compel arbitration before the issue was submitted to the arbitrator. Here, Petitioners request that the Court determine whether class arbitration is available after the parties voluntarily submitted the issue to the arbitrator. Additionaly, there is no authority that Garden Fresh can be retroactively applied. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr
